items.prompt: Quilt is best described in this part as supporting:
choices: Package-style dataset catalogs, browsing, and versioned sharing for teams
Exclusive replacement of all CI systems
items.explain: Quilt emphasizes collaborative package catalogs and versioned access.

items.prompt: DataHub is highlighted for documenting and discovering evolving real-time feed versions across teams.
items.explain: Example 8.23 centers on cataloged, versioned feeds for downstream consumers.

items.prompt: Git LFS is most appropriate when:
choices: Files are tiny text configs only
Large binaries must version beside Git without bloating the repository history
Teams refuse cloud or external object storage
items.explain: LFS keeps pointers in Git and stores large content externally.

items.prompt: Name one limitation of Git LFS relative to DVC-style workflows.
items.rubric: Mentions that LFS tracks files, not full data pipelines or rich dataset metadata/catalogs.
items.sample_answer: Git LFS versions large files but does not define executable data pipelines or rich dataset catalogs like DVC or Quilt.

items.prompt: Organizations often combine tools because no single system covers every catalog, pipeline, and large-file need.
items.explain: The comparison table and narrative encourage matching tools to jobs, sometimes in combination.
